verdict: The occupation of Judge is at high risk of displacement by AI, with a score of 80 indicating that 80% of tasks can be automated by current frontier models.

reasoning_steps.title: High routine_cognitive score
reasoning_steps.body: Task 1 (sentence defendants in criminal cases), Task 6 (rule on admissibility of evidence) and Task 8 (award compensation for damages) are all pattern-matching tasks that can be solved by current frontier models.

reasoning_steps.title: Large ai_capability_gap
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 1, 3, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10, and 11 can already be performed by AI tools with high accuracy.

adjacent_careers.name: Lawyer (Litigator)
adjacent_careers.reasoning: While some tasks like research and writing are still unique to lawyers, the increasing use of AI in legal research and document review may reduce the demand for human lawyers.

adjacent_careers.name: Paralegal
adjacent_careers.reasoning: As AI takes over more routine tasks like document review and research, paralegals may focus more on high-level tasks that require human judgment and expertise.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered legal research tool launched
recent_developments.impact: This new tool may reduce the need for human researchers in the legal field.

recent_developments.headline: AI contract review reaches enterprise adoption
recent_developments.impact: Top-100 law firms report cutting document review hours by 60–80% on standard agreements.

quotes.quote: Lawyers who use AI will replace lawyers who do not. The profession is not going away, but the structure of how legal work gets done is going to change in a very significant way.
quotes.speaker: Richard Susskind
quotes.title: Author, Tomorrow's Lawyers; technology adviser to the Lord Chief Justice of England

quotes.quote: Document review, due diligence, and first-draft contract work are the parts of legal practice most exposed. Counseling and courtroom judgment are the most insulated.
quotes.speaker: Daniel Susskind
quotes.title: Author, A World Without Work; Oxford economist

quotes.quote: We expect roughly 44 percent of legal work tasks to be automatable by current LLMs. That does not mean 44 percent fewer lawyers — it means restructured firms.
quotes.speaker: Goldman Sachs Research
quotes.title: Economic research note
quotes.source: Goldman Sachs: AI Exposure of Occupations
